title: Periodic re-audit of existing skills (trust decay for previously-approved skills)
description: HARD RULE 4 currently runs audit_skill() only on *newly generated or imported* skills. Once a skill passes, it receives permanent implicit trust. XZ Utils shows this is the wrong model: legitimate trust history is itself the attack surface. A skill approved 6 months ago under one version of soul_manager could silently drift — or a compromised upstream sync could replace it — without triggering a re-audit. Add a periodic re-audit pass to soul_manager that checksums all existing skill files, compares against stored hashes, and re-runs the security audit on any skill whose content has changed since last audit.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: 'legitimate trust relationships are the attack surface, not the defense.' Mira's current model treats audit as a one-time gate at ingestion — exactly the model XZ exploited. A skill file that passed audit at ingestion has earned 'two years of good behavior' status and is never questioned again. The fix is to make trust time-bounded: store a sha256 hash + audit timestamp per skill at approval time, then during self_evolve (daily 13-16 window) re-audit any skill whose file hash has changed or whose last_audited timestamp is older than a configurable threshold (e.g., 30 days).
diff_description: In soul_manager.py: (1) When audit_skill() approves a skill, write a sidecar entry in a skill_audit_log (JSON file in soul/) recording {skill_name, file_hash, audited_at}. (2) Add reaudit_stale_skills(max_age_days=30) that iterates all skill files in soul/skills/, computes current sha256, compares to stored hash — if hash changed OR last_audited > max_age_days, re-runs audit_skill(); if it fails, BLOCKS the skill (rename to .quarantined) and logs the incident. In core.py: call soul_manager.reaudit_stale_skills() inside the self_evolve dispatch handler, before any skill loading occurs in that cycle.
source_note: 2026-04-05_1-xz-utils攻击向量是两年的好人记录.md
